pi_items.source: Task description (line 2 mentions 'feed named `annex_power`'), setup_task.sh cleanup logic (line 18), export_result.sh feed query (line 31), verifier.py feed existence check (line 49-51)
Task description (line 2 mentions 'input key `power`'), setup_task.sh cleanup logic (line 28), export_result.sh input query (line 16), verifier.py input check (line 42-46)
Task description (line 2 mentions 'node name `building_annex`'), setup_task.sh cleanup logic (lines 18, 28), export_result.sh queries (line 16), verifier.py feedback (line 44)

setup_task.sh generates exactly 144 points (line 46: 'for i in range(144)'), with 600-second (10-minute) intervals, spanning 24 hours (86400 seconds). Python script execution confirms 144 total points. Verifier.py uses 140 as minimum threshold (line 61: 'if count >= 140')

Task description (line 2 mentions 'CSV file at `/home/ga/annex_power_data.csv`'), setup_task.sh (line 69 creates file: '> /home/ga/annex_power_data.csv'), with CSV header 'timestamp,power_watts' (line 45)

Task description (line 3 mentions 'save the final data point count to `/home/ga/annex_import_result.txt`'), export_result.sh (line 92 defines RESULT_PATH), result format is 'feed_name:annex_power,datapoints:NNN'

Verifier.py (line 54) checks for engine=='5' which is PHPFina. OpenEnergyMonitor documentation confirms PHPFina is the fixed-interval engine. Export_result.sh (line 35) fetches FEED_ENGINE from database. Setup scripts in environment use engine=5 for seed data (setup_emoncms.sh line 281-286)

Task description (line 2 mentions '10-minute intervals'), setup_task.sh (line 46-47: 'for i in range(144)' and 'ts = start_time + (i * 600)' confirms 600-second intervals). Python code execution verifies all 144 intervals are exactly 600 seconds
Task description (line 2 mentions '24 hours'), setup_task.sh (line 42-43: 'end_time - (144 * 600)' = 86400 seconds). Python code execution confirms (144 points × 600 seconds = 86400 seconds exactly)

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data generated by Python algorithm
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh (lines 36-69) contains embedded Python script that generates CSV data algorithmically with time-of-day power patterns. No external URLs or downloads. Data includes base load (300W), morning ramp (6-9 AM), day plateau (9-17 PM, 1800W), evening drop (17-22), and night baseline. Not from real-world dataset

privileged_info_summary: The task requires importing 144 synthetic power readings at 10-minute intervals (24 hours total) into Emoncms, a Docker-based energy monitoring system. Data is procedurally generated with realistic patterns and stored in CSV format at /home/ga/annex_power_data.csv. The task uses Emoncms node 'building_annex' with input 'power', configured to log to PHPFina feed 'annex_power' (engine ID 5). All metadata values are accurate and match the implementation: expected feed name is 'annex_power', input name is 'power', node name is 'building_annex', minimum expected data points are 140 (with 144 generated), CSV path is '/home/ga/annex_power_data.csv', and result file path is '/home/ga/annex_import_result.txt' with format 'feed_name:annex_power,datapoints:NNN'.
